verses.text: In His anger the Lord has covered Zion with a cloud and cast down 'the beauty of Israel.' He cut off her strength. When God turns foe, no glory can stand.
In His anger the Lord has covered Zion with a cloud and cast down 'the beauty of Israel.' He cut off her strength. When God turns foe, no glory can stand.
In His anger the Lord has covered Zion with a cloud and cast down 'the beauty of Israel.' He cut off her strength. When God turns foe, no glory can stand.
He has bent His bow like an enemy and poured His fury like fire, destroying His own places of assembly. The judgment is His. Even the holy courts are not spared when sin has filled them.
He has bent His bow like an enemy and poured His fury like fire, destroying His own places of assembly. The judgment is His. Even the holy courts are not spared when sin has filled them.
He has bent His bow like an enemy and poured His fury like fire, destroying His own places of assembly. The judgment is His. Even the holy courts are not spared when sin has filled them.
He has cast off His altar and sanctuary; the law is gone, and her prophets find no vision from the LORD. The means of grace are withdrawn. The heaviest stroke is when God ceases to speak.
He has cast off His altar and sanctuary; the law is gone, and her prophets find no vision from the LORD. The means of grace are withdrawn. The heaviest stroke is when God ceases to speak.
He has cast off His altar and sanctuary; the law is gone, and her prophets find no vision from the LORD. The means of grace are withdrawn. The heaviest stroke is when God ceases to speak.
The elders sit silent in the dust; children faint in the streets, crying to their mothers for bread. Helpless suffering. The innocent share the anguish that sin has brought upon all.
The elders sit silent in the dust; children faint in the streets, crying to their mothers for bread. Helpless suffering. The innocent share the anguish that sin has brought upon all.
The elders sit silent in the dust; children faint in the streets, crying to their mothers for bread. Helpless suffering. The innocent share the anguish that sin has brought upon all.
What can compare to her wound? Her prophets saw 'vain and foolish things,' not exposing her sin to turn her. False comfort ruined her. Teachers who flatter instead of warning destroy the people.
What can compare to her wound? Her prophets saw 'vain and foolish things,' not exposing her sin to turn her. False comfort ruined her. Teachers who flatter instead of warning destroy the people.
Passers-by clap and hiss, but the LORD has done what He purposed and 'fulfilled his word.' The enemy is only His rod. What befalls is the keeping of God's long-spoken warning.
Passers-by clap and hiss, but the LORD has done what He purposed and 'fulfilled his word.' The enemy is only His rod. What befalls is the keeping of God's long-spoken warning.
Passers-by clap and hiss, but the LORD has done what He purposed and 'fulfilled his word.' The enemy is only His rod. What befalls is the keeping of God's long-spoken warning.

Let the heart cry to the Lord, tears running down day and night; 'pour out thine heart like water before the face of the Lord' for thy children. Grief drives to prayer. Sorrow's right outlet is the throne of God.

Let the heart cry to the Lord, tears running down day and night; 'pour out thine heart like water before the face of the Lord' for thy children. Grief drives to prayer. Sorrow's right outlet is the throne of God.
She lays the horrors before God — mothers eating their children, priest and prophet slain in the sanctuary. Unbearable woe. Yet she brings even this to the LORD, holding nothing back.
